entry.title: Re: Using LPD_CUST to create launch page for FPM obsolete?
entry.content:
Hi folks, LPD_CUST is DEPRECATED since SAP S/4HANA 2020 and not yet obsolete. 

Deprecated means it still exists.  You should NOT create new entries using this technique. Refer to SAP Note 3013131 - Deprecation of LPD_CUST target mapping type for Fiori

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Stock Calculation in SAP Datasphere: Handling Non-Cumulative Stock Movements | S/4 IM
entry.content:
very useful thanks - Liking the reference to the views that S4 has provided

we are trying to move away from a SAP BW model to a Datasphere model (actually they want a passthrough to a datalake but im not sure this is feasable)

The issue we have is we have vast amounts of stock data and they want granular detail.

Qs

1) would the above models contain the movement types as we use these on a large scale within reports. It just looks like aggregated movements and a detail stock balance. Basically location, day, material and a bit more. We need mkpf/mseg data 

2) we currently have the marker principle in BW where we can move the marker - so we don't have to go too far back to the stock snapshot when adding movements (to reach a current stock figure). How do we cope with marker updates in datasphere? 

 

thanks very much

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Request to Duplicate Business Unit in SAP CDP
entry.content:
Duplication of Business units in sense to copy structured data at the moment is not supported in CDP.

In order to create same Business unit from existing Business unit there is option of import and export packages.

For example, your team can export structured data from Business unit PROD in JSON file and then import this file on Business unit DEV.

More details are available in documentation:

Import and export packages

KBA 3292363

entry.title: Re: Using HANA SQL Tabular Function in SQL Join
entry.content:
Dear @che_eky,
 
I did tests in my local system, HANA supports table function in JOIN.
Here are the steps and the SQLs I used during my test for your reference.
 
1. create a column store table: PRODUCTSALES
---------------------------------
CREATE ROW TABLE "I311040"."PRODUCTSALES" ( "PRODNAME" VARCHAR(50) CS_STRING,
"TYPE" VARCHAR(20) CS_STRING,
"SALES" INT CS_INT ) 
---------------------------------  
 
2. insert a few entries.
---------------------------------
PRODNAME;TYPE;SALES
Tee Shirt;Plain;21
Tee Shirt;Lettered;22
Tee Shirt;Team logo;30
Hoodie;Plain;60
Hoodie;Lettered;65
Hoodie;Team logo;80
Ballcap;Plain;8
Ballcap;Lettered;40
Ballcap;Team logo;27
---------------------------------
 
3. create table function.
---------------------------------
CREATE FUNCTION "I311040"."GET_ITEMS_BY_NAME" (
    IN name NVARCHAR(60)
)
RETURNS TABLE (
    PRODNAME NVARCHAR(60),
    SALES NVARCHAR(60)
)
LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
AS
BEGIN
    RETURN SELECT PRODNAME, SALES
    FROM PRODUCTSALES
    WHERE PRODNAME = :name;
END;
---------------------------------
 
4. JOIN the table function in SELECT statement.
---------------------------------
SELECT
    P.PRODNAME,
    I.SALES
FROM
    PRODUCTSALES P
JOIN
    "I311040"."GET_ITEMS_BY_NAME"('Hoodie') I
ON
    P.PRODNAME = I.PRODNAME;
---------------------------------
 
Result: see attachment "Charles Test result.png".
 
Hope it helps.
 
Best regards,
Charles

entry.title: Re: SAP ChaRM - Configure the action to read the type of...
entry.content:
@miksoneee wrote:

Hi,

Could someone please tell me how I can configure the step/action, if transport = customizing, will automatically jump from step 3 (To Be Tested) to step 6 (Imported to QA) skipping the additional approvals required to import the transport to QA. (Step 4 - QA Approval Required, Step 5 - QA Movement Approved). I would like this step to check the type of transport and then decide if additional approvals will be required. If transport = workbench additional approvals will be required.

BR,
Szymon

To make Customizing transports (TRs) skip QA approval steps in SAP ChaRM, you need to configure your workflow to conditionally jump from "To Be Tested" (Step 3) directly to "Imported to QA" (Step 6). This is achieved by defining conditions, likely using BRFplus, that check the transport type: if it's a Customizing transport, the system bypasses the "QA Approval Required" (Step 4) and "QA Movement Approved" (Step 5) steps, while Workbench transports will still follow the full approval process. This involves setting up automated actions in ChaRM's status profile, linked to these BRFplus conditions, to trigger the direct import for Customizing TRs.

entry.title: Blog It Forward - Masashi Otsuka(sumikko)
entry.content:
Please scroll down for the English version.
This post has a lot of pictures so please take a look.

こんにちは、SAP Communityの皆さん！
河原哲也さん @tetsuyakawahara  からご紹介いただき、今回「Blog It Forward」に参加させていただくことになりました。
※Blog It Forwardのことは以前から知っていましたが、まさか自分に回ってくるとは思っていませんでした

この場を借りて、私のSAPとの関わりやコミュニティ活動についてご紹介させていただきます。

 

自己紹介

名前：大塚將司（Masashi Otsuka）
ニックネーム：すみっこ
役割：S/4HANA拡張領域のコンサルタント／開発者
SAP歴：ABAP開発から始まり、現在はSAP BTPやAWSを活用したソリューション開発に従事しています。

2024年には、SAPジャパンより「SAP BTP Champion」として表彰されました。
この賞は、SAP BTPを活用した革新的な取り組みや、コミュニティへの貢献が評価されたものです。

 


人生を変えた2つのきっかけ

私の人生が大きく変わったきっかけは、2つあります。

(1)ブログ「SAPのすみっこ」をはじめ、コミュニティ「chillSAP」を立ち上げたこと


技術的な知見やイベントの記録を発信する中で、思いがけず多くの方に読んでいただけるようになり、社内外から声をかけてもらう機会が増えました。書くことで自分の考えが整理され、発信することで新たな出会いが生まれ、「情報を発信すると情報が集まってくる」「発信は自分を変える力がある」と実感しました。

そして同じようにSNSで発信している人たちと「もっと自由に、もっと楽しくSAPを語れる場を作りたい」という思いから始めたchillSAP。
そこには、年齢も職種も企業も超えて、SAPに関わるあらゆる人が集まりました。
この活動を通じて、本当に多くの人とつながることができ、人生の景色が変わったと感じています。

「chillSAP」は、SAPに関わるすべての人が「ゆるく、楽しく、つながる」ことを目的とした日本発のコミュニティです。
2019年に設立し、現在までに多数のイベントを開催してきました。

イベント：SAPなんでもライトニングトーク祭
SAPに関する話題なら何でもOKの5分間のLT大会。自由な発表スタイルが特徴です。

 

SAP Inside Track Tokyoの開催
2019年からsitTokyoの運営にも携わっています。他国の詳細はあまり把握しきれていないですが、Tokyoはセッション数は世界でもトップクラスなのでは？と思っています。
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2019 finished a great success #Recap #sitTokyo 
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2023 
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2024 イベントサイト 
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Information 

過去動画もYoutubeで公開していますので、ぜひご覧ください。

ちなみに、私もたまに登壇しています。2025年は「ABAP developer capabilities with Joule」をテーマにお話しさせていだきました。よろしければご覧ください。

 

(2)SAP TechEdに参加して刺激的な学びと出会いがあったこと

SAP TechEdは、私にとって技術と人とのつながりを再確認する場です。これまでに複数回参加し、毎回多くの刺激を受けてきました。

2018年
TechEd Las Vegasにブロガーとして初参加
ブログを始めてわずか8か月で、SAPジャパンから招待されて参加。
「まさか自分が」と驚きつつも、現地での体験がその後のキャリアと人生を大きく変えるきっかけに。

2019年
TechEd Las Vegasで「勝手にすみっこNight」開催
自主的に日本人参加者を集めて交流会を開催。企業や立場を超えてつながる場を自ら作り出した経験は、コミュニティ活動の原点のひとつです

2023年
TechEd Bangaloreに現地参加
コロナ後初のオンサイト開催。SAP BuildやAI関連セッションの実践的なデモや現地参加者の熱量に感動。
世界中の開発者と直接交流できたことが、技術的にも人間的にも大きな刺激に。



日本で開催されたSAP TechEd Japanのクロージングセッションでパネリストとして登壇させていただきました

 

2024年
TechEd on Tour @メルボルン（SAUG-x 2024）
空いていて快適な環境で、じっくりとセッションに集中することができました。
SAPジャパンによる日本人向けサポートも充実しており、安心して参加できた。

 

 

 

 

TechEdを通じたモチベーションの再充電
「これがあるから1年頑張れる」と思うほど、TechEdは私にとって心のチャージの場。
SNSやYouTubeで見ていたSAP Communityの有名な皆さんたちのセッションを生で体験できたことは、今でも忘れられません。
私と写真を撮った記憶がある方はいますか？🙋

 

 

 

BIF質問への回答

自分が20歳に戻ったら何を勉強したい？
英語とIT基礎です。これらをしっかりと学ぶべきだったと思っています(いま勉強中です)

両親から学んだベストレッスン
機嫌のいい両親から学んだことは、「自分の機嫌は自分でとる」ということです。

 

次のBlog It Forward参加者

私からは、日本で一緒にコミュニティを運営しているみのさん( @Mino )  にバトンを渡したいと思います。みのさんの知的好奇心の持ち方と信念がブレないところに私は常に刺激をもらっています。
みのさんの BIF ブログを、ぜひお楽しみに！

 

最後に

SAPのすみっこブログから発信を始めて、今では多くの方とつながることができました。これからもコミュニティを盛り上げていきたいと思います。Blog It Forwardにも参加できて光栄です。
ここまで読んでいただき、ありがとうございました！

以下は、私が2018年に初めて社外で登壇した際の写真です




--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hello, SAP Community!

Thanks to the introduction from Mr. Tetsuya Kawahara @tetsuyakawahara  , I’m excited to be participating in the Blog It Forward  initiative.

I’ve known about Blog It Forward for a while, but I never imagined it would come around to me!

I’d like to take this opportunity to share a bit about my involvement with SAP and my activities within the community.

 

About me

Name: Masashi Otsuka
Nickname: Sumikko
Role: Consultant/Developer in the S/4HANA Extension Area
SAP Experience: I started with ABAP development and am currently engaged in solution development utilizing SAP BTP and AWS.

In 2024, I was honored by SAP Japan as an SAP BTP Champion.
This award recognizes innovative initiatives leveraging SAP BTP and contributions to the community.

 


Two Turning Points That Changed My Life

There are two major turning points that significantly changed the course of my life.

(1) Starting the blog “SAP Sumikko” and launching the community “chillSAP”

 

While sharing technical insights and event recaps through my blog, I was pleasantly surprised to find that many people started reading it. This led to more opportunities to connect with others, both inside and outside my company. Writing helped me organize my thoughts, and sharing them brought new encounters. I came to truly realize that “sharing information attracts more information” and “sharing has the power to transform you.”

Inspired by others who were also actively sharing on social media, I launched chillSAP with the desire to create a space where people could talk about SAP more freely and enjoyably.

chillSAP brought together people from all backgrounds—regardless of age, profession, or company—united by their connection to SAP. Through this activity, I’ve been able to connect with so many people, and I feel that the entire landscape of my life has changed.

chillSAP is a Japan-born community with the goal of helping everyone involved with SAP connect in a relaxed and enjoyable way.
Founded in 2019, we’ve hosted numerous events to date.

Event Highlight: SAP Anything Lightning Talk Festival
A 5-minute lightning talk event where any SAP-related topic is welcome.
The presentations are characterized by their free and flexible style, allowing participants to share ideas in a relaxed and creative way.

 

Event Highlight: SAP Inside Track Tokyo

Since 2019, I’ve been involved in organizing SAP Inside Track Tokyo (sitTokyo).
Although I’m not fully familiar with the details of other countries’ events, I believe Tokyo ranks among the top globally in terms of the number of sessions.
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2019 finished a great success #Recap #sitTokyo 
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2023 
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2024 イベントサイト 
SAP Inside Track Tokyo 2025 Event Information 

We’ve also published recordings of past sessions on YouTube, so please feel free to check them out!
Youtube

By the way, I also occasionally speak at events.
In 2025, I gave a talk on the theme “ABAP Developer Capabilities with Joule.”
Please feel free to check it out if you're interested!
YouTube

 

(2) Attending SAP TechEd and Experiencing Inspiring Learning and Encounters

SAP TechEd has always been a place for me to reconnect with both technology and people.
I’ve attended multiple times, and each time has brought me fresh inspiration and motivation.

2018
I attended TechEd Las Vegas for the first time as a blogger.
Just eight months after starting my blog, I was invited by SAP Japan to join the event.
I was honestly surprised—“I can’t believe it’s me!”—but the experience on-site became a major turning point in both my career and my life.

2019
At TechEd Las Vegas, I hosted an informal meetup called “Sumikko Night.”
I took the initiative to gather Japanese participants and create a space for connection and conversation.
This experience of building a community space that transcended companies and roles became one of the foundations of my community activities.

2023
I attended TechEd Bangalore in person—the first on-site TechEd after the COVID-19 pandemic.
I was deeply impressed by the practical demos on SAP Build and AI-related sessions, as well as the energy and enthusiasm of the local participants.
Being able to directly interact with developers from around the world was incredibly stimulating, both technically and personally.



I also had the honor of speaking as a panelist during the closing session of SAP TechEd Japan, held in Tokyo.

2024
I participated in TechEd on Tour @ Melbourne (SAUG-x 2024).
The venue was spacious and comfortable, allowing me to fully concentrate on the sessions.
SAP Japan provided excellent support for Japanese attendees, which made the experience smooth and reassuring.

 

 

 

 

Recharging My Motivation Through TechEd

TechEd is a place that recharges my spirit—so much so that I feel, “This is what keeps me going for the year.”
Being able to attend sessions by well-known SAP Community members, whom I had only seen on social media or YouTube, was an unforgettable experience.

It was incredibly inspiring to meet these people in person and feel the energy of the global SAP community.
TechEd reminds me of the joy of learning and the power of connection—it’s a source of motivation that fuels me throughout the year.

Do any of you remember taking a photo with me? 🙋

 

 

 

Answers to Blog It Forward Questions

 

If you could go back to age 20, what would you study?
I would study English and IT fundamentals.
I believe I should have learned these properly back then—I'm currently studying them now!

Best lesson learned from your parents?
From my cheerful parents, I learned this valuable lesson:
“Take care of your own mood.”

 

Next Blog It Forward Participant

I’d like to pass the baton to Mino-san ( @Mino ), who co-manages the community with me here in Japan.
I’m always inspired by his intellectual curiosity and unwavering sense of purpose.
Please look forward to his Blog It Forward post!

 

In Closing

Starting with my blog SAP Sumikko, I’ve been fortunate to connect with many wonderful people.
I hope to continue contributing to and energizing the SAP community.
It’s truly an honor to be part of the Blog It Forward initiative.

Thank you so much for reading all the way through!

Below is a photo from my very first external speaking engagement in 2018.

entry.title: Clarification on UAE WPS Payment Processing Based on Sponsor ID
entry.content:
Dear Experts

We would appreciate your expertise, help, and support regarding the WPS processing configuration as per UAE regulations and company Group’s internal payroll model. Kindly take note of the following key requirements:

  • In the case of WPS payments, Company Group processes salary disbursements through sponsor-specific bank accounts, rather than the legal entity’s house bank.
  • Each sponsor is linked to a specific bank account, and a single legal entity may have multiple associated sponsors.
  • Therefore, WPS files must be generated based on the Sponsor ID, and not solely by company code.
  • If the Sponsor ID is the same as the Legal Entity ID, then the default house bank for the company code should be used.
  • For example, if a specific business unit has 100 employees across 5 different sponsors, the system should generate 5 separate WPS files, one for each sponsor along with sponsor bank details 
  • Sponsor bank account details are already maintained in the system to support this logic.

Summary of Required Logic:

  • Use Sponsor ID as the basis for WPS file generation.
  • Usi the Company Code’s house bank only when Sponsor ID = Legal Entity ID.

Thanks

Sameer

entry.title: Redesigned Applicant Workbench - "View Profile" Button Now Available!
entry.content:
We're Excited to Bring Back a Favorite Feature in the Redesigned Applicant Workbench!
Thanks to your valuable feedback, we’re pleased to announce a new update to the Redesigned Applicant Workbench—the return of a highly requested feature from the legacy workbench.

The "View Profile" deep link is back!
You can now easily access a candidate’s profile directly from the application detail page, making your navigation smoother and your recruiting workflow more efficient.

This enhancement was driven by customer input through the Influence Portal, where more than 80 customers voted to bring this feature back. We heard you—and we delivered.

Stay tuned for more updates, and thank you for helping us improve your recruiting experience!

Helpful Resources

  • SAP Help Guide: The Redesigned Applicant Management Tool

entry.title: What's New in SAP BTP – Q2 2025
entry.content:
Q2 was another big quarter for SAP Business Technology Platform. From application development to integration and analytics, we are excited to share how SAP BTP continues to simplify, streamline, and speed things up! Let's dive into some of the top innovations!

App Dev & Automation Highlights 

Simplified SAPUI5 extensions with the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard 

If your business relies on Cloud ERP as its IT backbone, you know it may need extensions to meet unique requirements. When built-in extensibility isn’t enough, SAP Build on SAP BTP offers powerful options for custom coding and Fiori UI extensions.  

Now, with the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, you can create UI extensions directly from your Cloud ERP application. By launching the Extensibility Wizard directly from your SAP Fiori application, you are given different options for the type of extension you want to create.

The User Interface Extension lets you either adapt the existing Fiori app or create a new custom application. By setting up your destinations and details in the Wizard, you can either remove sections, add new fields, and create a completely new variant of the application with SAP Build Code and Business Application Studio. Developers and key users can already use it to create custom processes and now, custom user interfaces. 

Watch the video demo (starting at 0:08)  for more insights!   

Learn more:  Simplify SAPUI5 Code Extensions with the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard

Application to Enable Business Users to Manage Decisions

To empower business users to view or maintain decisions within SAP Build Process Automation, we launched a dedicated, purpose-built application that can be accessed directly from SAP Build Work Zone – without the need of IT. 

Business users can access a dedicated application in SAP Build Work Zone to manage decisions within a single project. Users can open the decision table, modify values directly, or import data from Excel and then save their changes, seeing only what matters and making the experience clean and focused. The release of a changed version is only one click away. Any errors are shown in the design console, letting users view the different versions of the project. 

See for yourself in the demo (starting at 2:05)  for more insights!

Learn more 

  • Configure the Stuido Home Application 
  • Using the Studio Home Application

Partner Solutions as an Installable Product on SAP BTP ABAP Environment 

Good news for SAP partners developing apps with SAP BTP ABAP Environment: you can now offer your solution not only as a multitenant Software as a Service solution, but also as an installable product! 

Customers can now install the solution directly in their system as a product. As a part of SAP BTP ABAP Environment, the Landscape Portal supports both delivery options: multitenant SaaS and installable product. 

You can do this in three easy steps:

  1. Build your product with the Register Product app. 
  2. Choose your delivery model: 
    • For multitenant SaaS, configure it in the Maintain Solution app  
    • For installable product, use the Publish Product app. 
  3. Publish your solution to the SAP Store with a PartnerEdge Build contract and an SAP Store enhancement. 

Now customers using SAP BTP ABAP Environment can find your product in the SAP Store and install it into their own system using the Deploy Product option. 

For detailed guidance, watch the demo (starting at 3:42). 

Learn more: Building partner products on SAP BTP ABAP Environment

Integration Highlight

Describe Externalized Parameters of an Integration Flow 

In SAP Integration Suite externalization enables integration developers to define parameters as variables for certain configurations of adapters or flow steps of an integration flow. The values of these parameters can be changed without editing the integration flow, for example, when moving integrations from development or test environment to production. To simplify the understanding of this difficult and technical process, content developers can now provide descriptions directly in the integration flow editor. These can be the potential values, examples or additional details explaining the impact of specific values which will then be visible for guiding through each of the fields in the Configure view. This brings huge potential to improve the maintainability and reusability of your integration scenarios.  

Take a look at the demo (starting at 5:21) and see for yourself! 

Learn more: Externalized Parameters View

Data & Analytics Highlight

Analytical insights in Joule 

Joule, SAP's copilot, now enables you to get quick analytical answers without having to build reports. This provides you with valuable insights, saving time and giving you the information you need. 

Joule scans available data sources and suggests a few options, making comparisons to previous months easier. Joule updates the view right away, making it easy to see how things are trending. For example, you can ask for the top ten sales agents, check the monthly commission trend from January to March, giving you contextual and relevant answers 

See this in action in the demo (starting 7:15).

Learn more: Introducing Analytical Insights in Joule: Empowering Smarter Decisions, Instantly

Cross Platform Highlight

Provision App-Specific Groups in Identity Directory 

SAP Cloud Identity Services tenant enables immediate provisioning of authorizations to target systems in SAP Analytics Cloud target system. You can  centrally manage user-group assignments linked to application-specific policies, triggering provisioning jobs that synchronize access rights efficiently and securely. This ensures users gain prompt access to their designated roles in the target system.

Watch the demo (starting at 8:37) for a closer look. 

Learn more: SAP Cloud Identity Services

Share your thoughts 

These videos are for YOU. We would love to hear your impressions. How do you like the video? How can we improve the upcoming videos? Please share your feedback by liking this post or leaving a comment below.

Thank you for your support and happy viewing, sharing, and enjoying!

You Might Also Be Interested In

  • SAP BTP Garage: monthly LIVE webinar with hands-on use case walkthroughs and demos.
  • SAP BTP Talk Podcast: monthly podcast covering all things SAP BTP.

entry.title: Re: Opening &and Closing Inventory IFP Calculation
entry.content:
Hi,

Are looking for the standard data action name specifically calculating inventory in standard content or useful information to calculate inventory technically with the logic you described? Carryforward function can help if you look for data action set up guidance. Please check this:

 https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/afe93e3cf1414a7b8419baad11cc066e.html

In a model with measures, you may want to use measures in your carry forward function. This lets you set up different exception aggregation types over time for the carry forward measures, for example FIRST for the opening measure and LAST for the closing measure. For example: Sample Code DATA() = CARRYFORWARD([d/Measures], "Opening", "Closing", "Opening" + "Increase" - "Decrease")

entry.title: Re: DFKKZP-KLAEB updated with value ************ and DFKKZP-OPBEL updated with value *
entry.content:
Ankit, 

There are two flows here-

If the payment has been posted to Account via the payment clarification, it will always go back to payment clarification once you reverse it from the account. and then you want have to reverse it out from the the clarification to reverse the payment.

If the payment is posted directly to the account, then once you reverse it, you get a pop up whether you want to reverse it directly or send it back to Clarification, if you choose reverse directly the payment is reversed and there is no impact on status of payment lot. However if you choose the other option it will  send the payment back to CWL.

So I hope you are going by option 1 here and thats how you are getting it.

Hope it clears the data flow.

Thanks

Amlan

entry.title: Re: Refresh table after execute bound action
entry.content:
Hello @JastinC1 

Thank you for your message.

The bound actions do update the data, but the UI does not automatically refresh filtered table views in S4HANA Public cloud. This is a known limitation in Fiori Elements apps.

Although side effects and $self return values are correctly configured, the current table won’t remove the updated record unless manually refreshed or re-bound. Unfortunately, custom UI refresh logic isn’t supported in standard apps on the public cloud.

We recommend using proper filters on the view and asking users to refresh or switch tabs to see the change. If needed, we can explore building a custom UI with Developer Extensibility for better control.

Check this link! https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-development-tools-user-guide/abap-profiling?version=s4hana_cloud

Best regards,
Chuma

entry.title: Re: What is the name of the CDS view to check commitment...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Can you please check whether the following information solves your issue?

CDS Views for Commitment Values Linked to WBS Elements

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, you can explore various CDS views to find commitment values linked to WBS elements. Here are some relevant CDS views:

  1. I_WBSElementBasicData Virtual Data Model and CDS Views - Basic WBS Element Data

    • This view provides organizational details associated with a WBS element, including controlling area, company code, and profit center.

  2. I_EntWBSElementHierarchy Virtual Data Model and CDS Views - WBS Element Hierarchy

    • This view retrieves the header information of the WBS element hierarchy for enterprise projects and professional services projects.

  3. I_EntProjElmntWorkItem Virtual Data Model and CDS Views - Work Item of Enterprise Project Element

    • This view retrieves work item details of project elements, which can include commitment values from purchase requisitions (PRs) and purchase orders (POs).

These views can be used to analyze and report on commitment values associated with WBS elements in your enterprise projects.

[1] Virtual Data Model and CDS Views - Basic WBS Element Data
[2] Virtual Data Model and CDS Views - WBS Element Hierarchy
[3] Virtual Data Model and CDS Views - Work Item of Enterprise Project Element

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Scope Transfer from SAP Business ByDesign to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Using Digital Discovery Assistant
entry.content:
Overview: 

 

Organizations moving from SAP Business ByDesign (ByD) to SAP S/4HANA Cloud often face the challenge of realigning their existing business scope with the new cloud-native solution. To simplify this process, SAP offers a digital accelerator: the Digital Discovery Assessment (DDA) tool, which now supports scope transfer by uploading the Business Adaptation Catalog(BAC) file exported from SAP Business ByDesign.

In this blog, we’ll walk through how this feature supports customers in their journey—and why it’s a game-changer for implementation efficiency and accuracy.

 

Key Features:

  • Upload-Based Scope Mapping: Directly upload your Business Adaptation Catalog file from SAP Business ByDesign to DDA.
  • Automated Scope Suggestion: The DDA automatically suggests suitable scope items for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud based on your current ByD configuration.
  • Expert-Backed Logic: The underlying mapping has been curated by qualified consultants with deep expertise in both SAP Business ByDesign and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
  • Accelerated Fit-to-Standard Preparation: Significantly reduces the time required to prepare for the Fit-to-Standard workshops during the implementation phase.

How It Works:

Step 1: Exporting the Business Adaptation Catalogue (BAC) File from SAP Business ByDesign

Begin by exporting the Business Adaptation Catalog file from your SAP Business ByDesign system. This file contains all the necessary configuration data that will be used in the next steps.

  1. Navigate to the Business Configuration work center.
  2. Go to Implementation projects and select the latest Project 
  3. Edit the scope and navigate to Step 4
  4. Select the "Export" => Microsoft Excel
  5. Save the file to your local system.

Note: If the Import fails, then Open the exported file in Microsoft excel and perform the Save Operation (current workaround)

 

Step 2: Uploading the BAC File to the Digital Discovery Assessment (DDA)

Next, upload the exported Business Adaptation Catalog file to the DDA. The DDA is a powerful tool that helps in identifying the relevant scope items for your new SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system.

  1. Access the DDA tool and start a new Assessment
  2. Complete the steps the Step 1 and Step 2. Select the option Installed base and choose New Implementation for the Question 3 in  Step 2A
  3. After you Add & Qualify the Opportunity, you should be able to Import the BAC under Import and Export tab in Import SAP Business ByDesign(ByD) Configuration - SAP Cloud Transition Factory section.

 

Step 3: Reviewing the Suggested Scope Items

Once the file is uploaded, the DDA will analyze the configuration data and propose relevant scope items. These proposal are based on the expertise of qualified consultants from both ByD and S/4HANA, ensuring that the transition is as seamless as possible.

  1. Review the suggested scope items.
  2. Make any necessary adjustments based on your specific business needs.
  3. Confirm the scope items to proceed with the transition.

Behind the Scenes

The scope mapping logic is the result of extensive collaboration between consultants specializing in SAP Business ByDesign and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Their functional expertise ensures that the recommendations are:

  • Relevant and practical – tailored for real-world transition scenarios
  • Continuously updated – as new scope items and functionalities are released in S/4HANA Cloud
  • Aligned with industry best practices – supporting a smoother project start and faster value realization

This is the Baseline S4H Scope coverage fitting for Transition, there might be differences based on the Customer Industry or adoption of the solution. We are only mapping the standard process to the Baseline(no additional fee), non-baseline with non additional fee and finally with non-baseline with additional fee . Partner/Project functional consultants who are more aware of the Customer need to accept/veto the scope mapping based on Customer need/adoption potential of the S4H Public cloud solution. We propose this as a accelerator potentially.

Why It Matters: From Configuration to Realization

Here’s how this feature adds value for customers:

  • Faster Scoping: Skip hours of manual scoping by letting DDA analyze your existing setup.
  • Reduced Risk: Reliably align existing business processes with supported S/4HANA Cloud scope items.
  • Guided Transformation: Move from ERP concepts in ByD (like work centers and activity sets) to scope items and fit-to-standard in S/4HANA Cloud.
  • Accelerated Fit-to-Standard: Start implementation workshops with a pre-defined, pre-mapped scope that reflects your current business practices.

Start your transformation journey with confidence—leverage this accelerator to bridge the gap between SAP Business ByDesign and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. We're excited to hear your feedback and continuously improve this offering based on real customer experiences.

entry.title: SAP PaPM Post-Installation – ABAP Steps
entry.content:
Introduction 

Hello everyone, 

Have you ever tried buying a new phone that includes a manual that teaches you how to set up the device? Did you try to follow it through and perform the steps provided? In PaPM, there is also a manual that will guide you through the steps required after the installation of SAP Profitability and Performance Management 3.0. This procedure is called post-installation and will help you ensure that the application works in the live client where the system is activated. 

We have three parts of post-installation: 

  • ABAP steps 
  • HANA steps 
  • Default environment steps 

You need to perform manual steps after a successful installation of SAP Profitability and Performance Management 3.0 or a support package update to ensure the application works consistently. 

 

ABAP Steps 

The ABAP steps you need to carry out depend on your individual situation: 
For a newly installed SAP Profitability and Performance Management 3.0 system, you need to perform the steps from Support Package 01 up to the latest patch level installed in the system. 

For a Support Package update, ensure you perform the steps mentioned in the newly implemented Support Packages. 

Note: If you were originally on SP07 and then move to SP19, you need to perform all the steps required for SP08, SP09, SP10, SP11, SP12, SP13, SP14, SP15, SP16, SP17, SP18, and SP19. 

 

Set Namespaces 

This step is a cross-client activity that can be performed in any active client within the system where SAP Profitability and Performance Management is installed. In SAP Profitability and Performance Management there are certain namespaces that need to be set to “modifiable” up until SP13. From SP14 onwards, this is no longer necessary and can be skipped  

Possible issues when not performed: 
You may encounter activation issues with the system prompting you to set the namespace to modifiable. You will also encounter issues during transport when after-import has initiated the activation/generation of objects.  

More information: 
PaPM is customizing. Activation in PaPM does not just involve SE38 inactive report -- it involves a creation of artifacts as well (function Ytables, procedures, process chains, queries, and so on). Therefore, ensure you follow the steps described in the Administration Guide under Set Namespaces. 

 

Establish Database Connection 

This step is a cross-client activity that can be performed in any active client within the system where SAP Profitability and Performance Management is installed. You can specify the database connection name within the environment settings in SAP Profitability and Performance Management.  

From SP22 onwards, you can make use of the DEFAULT database connection already available in Netweaver ABAP. You no longer need to create a DBCON database connection. All the sample content environments from SP22 onwards use the DEFAULT database connection. 

If you have already established the DBCON database connection for previous support packages in your environment, you can continue using them or you can change all your environments to use the DEFAULT connection. 

 

Create RFC Destination 

This step is a cross-client activity that can be performed in any active client within the system where SAP Profitability and Performance Management is installed. 

The default naming convention for the RFC destination is “<System_ID>CLNT<Client>”. For example, “PERCLNT100”, where “PER” is the system ID and “100” is the client on which you want to configure SAP Profitability and Performance Management functions. By following this naming convention, you will not need to apply any additional settings in the system. However, if your system landscape requires you to use a different name for the RFC destination, you need to register the new name in the Connection Management application with connection source “After Import”. For more information about maintaining connections to external systems, see the Connection Management section in the Application Help for SAP Profitability and Performance Management. 

Possible issues when not performed: 
You may encounter issues during transport.  

For more information, see Create RFC Destination. 

 

Create Local SAP System Alias 

Since SAP Profitability and Performance Management or NXI services need to be assigned to a local SAP Gateway named “LOCAL”, it is mandatory to have this created in the system where SAP Profitability and Performance Management is being activated. 

The system alias is the result of the routing for an inbound request on SAP Gateway and it can be a remote or a local system. If the system alias is flagged as a local SAP GW instance, it means that the system that is responsible for processing (managing and storing) the data of an inbound request is the local SAP Gateway instance itself. Therefore, testing this item is leaning more on connection settings related to activation of NXI services. 

For more information, see Creating an SAP System Alias. 

 

Create Semantic Object 

Semantic objects are delivered in the underlying SAP_UI component SPs. The semantic object of SAP Profitability and Performance Management will be delivered in SAP_UI 750 SP12. If your system is already patched to SAP_UI 750 SP12, the semantic object of SAP Profitability and Performance Management is automatically available. 

Note:  
If your system is on SAP_UI 750 SP10 or SAP_UI 750 SP11, you need to create the semantic object of SAP Profitability and Performance Management in every system where you want to use the SAP Fiori apps for SAP Profitability and Performance Management. 

For more information on the procedure, see Create Semantic Object. 

 

Activate Deep HANA Integration 

Note that in SAP BW/4HANA systems, Deep HANA integration is switched on by default. You can go to transaction SE11 and enter the table name RSPLS_HDB_ACT to see the entries. Since this is a cross-client activity, you can do this in any client within the system where SAP Profitability and Performance Management is installed. To do so, follow the procedure described in Activate Deep HANA Integration. 

Possible issues when not performed: 
The maintenance dialog for RSPLS_HDB_ACT is incomplete or not defined. 

Message no. SV037 

Diagnosis 
The called function with the view/table RSPLS_HDB_ACT needs a special maintenance dialog which, however, either does not exist at all, or does not exist completely. 

System Response 
The called function cannot be performed. 

 

Set Number Range Intervals 

This step must be performed for every client in the system where SAP Profitability and Performance Management is used. 

Before operating SAP Profitability and Performance Management, set the number range intervals for the following number range objects using transaction SNRO. Ensure that you enter “01” in the No. column (not just “1”). 

For the Process ID object, maintaining the number range interval is optional. It is only required if you want the system to suggest the process ID automatically during the creation of the process instance. 

Note: 
The number range intervals listed below are reserved for the SAP sample content. To avoid duplicate ID errors, make sure that the number range intervals listed below are not used: 

Possible issues when not performed: 
Transporting environments across systems/clients where number range interval definitions for /NXI/* number range objects are identical could lead to serious inconsistencies. When you are setting up the landscape, you therefore need to make sure that the number range intervals do not overlap and are unique. 

 

Activate All NXI Services 

This step is a cross-client setup that must be revisited for every support package update activity. In SAP Profitability and Performance Management, there are specific NXI services that need to be activated before you can use applications under Modeling, Execution, and System Reports. 

Note: 
Since PaPM is accessed via browser, these webdynpro, bsp, ui5_ui5, OData should all be activated. 

For more information on the procedure, see Activate All NXI Services. 

 

Assign SAP System Alias to NXI Services 

The local SAP system alias created in the previous step must be assigned to all NXI services. 

For more information on the procedure, see Assign SAP System Alias to NXI Services. 

Possible issues when not performed: 
You may encounter dump like “500 Internal Server Error” when launching My Environments or any PaPM application. 

 

Publish OData Services 

To integrate the processing service, you need to publish the service group following the steps in Publish OData Services. 

 

Adjust Maximum Record Length (RADTBCON viewlen) 

This step is a cross-client activity that can be performed in any active client within the system where SAP Profitability and Performance Management is installed. 

Note: 
If your system is on SAP_BASIS version 753 (for example, BW/4HANA 2.0 or S/4HANA 1909) and above, you can skip this step. 

If you intend to import the sample content or use SAP Profitability and Performance Management with a record length greater than 4030, perform the following steps in Adjust Maximum Record Length (RADTBCON viewlen). 

Possible issues when not performed: 
PaPM function table activation is failing when total length of all the fields exceeds more than 4030. Error: table Y032PUD000 is too long >4030 
msg: Y032PUD000 -> no 4030 -> no relevant 

 

Adjust Maximum Record Length (RADTBCON tablen) 

This step is a cross-client activity that can be performed in any active client within the system where SAP Profitability and Performance Management is installed. 

Note: 
If your system is on SAP_BASIS version 753 (for example, BW/4HANA 2.0 or S/4HANA 1909) and above, you can skip this step. 

If you intend to import the sample content or use SAP Profitability and Performance Management with a record length greater than 4030, perform the following steps in Adjust Maximum Record Length (RADTBCON tablen). 

 

Maintain User Groups 

To enable individual users to use the Manage and Deploy Processes application, you need to assign them to a user group. 

For more information, see Maintain User Groups. 

Possible issues when not performed: 
You will not be able to see the process instance and activities created.  

 

Migrate Models 

This step is valid when patching to SAP Profitability and Performance Management 3.0 SP04 onwards. 

An enhanced UI for models with sync and mapping functionalities is available. Run the migration report /NXI/P1_MIGRATE_MODELS to activate functions configured with the old UI. 

Proceed with the steps in Migrate Models. 

 

Set Up Dual Control for Usage in Process Execution (Optional) 

If you intend to use the dual control functionality (with performers and reviewers) including email notification when you run processes or activities, you need to set up the basic workflow and perform the SAPconnect administration or check that this has already been done. 

For more information about the basic workflow setup, see Automatic Workflow Customizing. 

 

Implement Important SAP Notes 

Users should be able to implement important SAP Notes. 

Possible issues when not performed: 
You may encounter issues when running the Writer function. 

You need this also to achieve the Editable Visual Modeler. 

For more information, see Implement Important SAP Notes. 

 

Create Tool BW Client 

To integrate SAP Profitability and Performance Management with BW, you can activate the BW client in the following scenarios: 

  • In a different client than SAP Profitability and Performance Management (for example, the BW is in client 100; SAP Profitability and Performance Management is in client 200, but in the same system) 
  • In the same client as SAP Profitability and Performance Management (for example, BW and SAP Profitability and Performance Management are both in client 200). 

For more information on the procedure, see Create Tool BW Client. 

Note: 
You need to recheck the RSADMINA table in transaction code SE16 of the BI Client and verify that BW_USER, BWMANDT, BWMANDTRFC, and TPBWMANDTRFC are properly populated. 

 

Adjust RSADMINA Table (Column TPBWMANDTRFC) 

In the live client for SAP Profitability and Performance Management, assign the BW RFC destination to the TPBWMANDTRFC column in the RSADMINA table. 

For more information on the procedure, see Adjust RSADMINA Table (Column TPBWMANDTRFC). 

Note: 
For additional information about how to modify the RSADMINA table, see SAP Notes 1949194 and 1911225. 

 

Create SAP HANA Source System 

There are two ways to create the source system:  

  • Via BW Client  
  • Via SAP HANA Studio 

For BW Client, follow the steps in Create SAP HANA Source System.  
For SAP HANA Studio, follow the steps in Create SAP HANA Source System.  

Possible issues when not performed: 
You may encounter issues like logical systems for a particular database schema does not exist. 

Note: 
If you configure the Model Table function and Model View function in the environments of SAP Profitability and Performance Management to point to sources from other schemas, you also need to carry out the steps listed above for every schema. 

 

Activate Planning Function Type 

This activity is to be performed in the client where BW has been activated. To do this, follow the procedure in Activate Planning Function Type. 

Possible issues when not performed: 
You may encounter issues with the Writer function or cannot create ADSO InfoProvider, Model BW.  

 

Activate Query Element Variables 

This activity needs to be performed in the client where BW has been activated. To do this, follow the procedure in Activate Planning Function Type. 

Now we are already done with the ABAP Steps, we can already proceed with the other blogpost that tackles the HANA and default environment steps. You can check it through this link.

entry.title: SAP PaPM Post-Installation – HANA Steps
entry.content:
Introduction 

The first part of this blog covers only the ABAP steps. In this second part, we will discuss the HANA steps and default environment steps. 

We have three parts of post-installation: 

  • ABAP steps 
  • HANA steps 
  • Default environment steps 

HANA Steps 

The steps in this section need to be performed in SAP HANA Studio. 

The HANA steps you need to carry out depend on your individual situation: 

  • For a newly installed SAP Profitability and Performance Management 3.0 system, you need to perform the steps from Support Package 01 up to the latest patch level installed in the system. 
  • For a Support Package update, ensure you perform the steps mentioned in the newly implemented Support Packages.  

Note: If you were originally on SP07 and then moved to SP19, you need to perform all the steps required for SP08, SP09, SP10, SP11, SP12, SP13, SP14, SP15, SP16, SP17, SP18, and SP19. 

 

Create NXI Schema in SAP HANA Studio 

The steps in this section need to be performed in SAP HANA Studio. Follow the steps in the procedure section in Create NXI Schema in SAP HANA Studio. 

Possible issues when not performed: 
You will not be able to activate all modeling functions as the required procedure cannot be generated if this step was not performed. 

 

Grant Full Authorization to SAP DB Connection User in SAP HANA Studio 

The steps in this section need to be performed in SAP HANA Studio Follow the steps in the procedure section in Grant Full Authorization to SAP DB Connection User in SAP HANA Studio. 

Possible issues when not performed: 
You may encounter activation issues where the procedure you need to run or activate is included in NXI schema. 

 

Grant SELECT and EXECUTE Authorizations to _SYS_REPO User in SAP HANA Studio 

The steps in this section need to be performed in SAP HANA Studio. Follow the steps in the procedure section in Grant SELECT and EXECUTE Authorizations to _SYS_REPO User in SAP HANA Studio. 

Possible issues when not performed: 
You may encounter authorization issues when activating functions like model views. 
 

Assign System Privileges to SAP DB Connection User 

Prerequisites 
Grant the system privilege CATALOG READ to the SAP DB Connection User in the SAP HANA database. 

Context 
Grant the following system privileges to the SAP DB Connection User in the SAP HANA database if you intend to use the following: 

A virtual table or view using the HANA SDA function type in the model view 

  • CREATE REMOTE SOURCE 
  • DATA ADMIN 

File Adapter function 

  • IMPORT 
  • EXPORT 

SAP HANA R Script function type in the remote function adapter 

  • CREATE R SCRIPT 

For more information on the procedure, see Assign System Privileges to SAP DB Connection User.  

 

Adjust SAP HANA DB Parameters 

If you intend to use the upload/download functionality of the File Adapter function, you need to adjust the following parameters in the SAP HANA database as described in Adjust SAP HANA DB Parameters.  

Possible issues when not performed: 
Activation/Run of file adapter function results in a generation error where the system cannot open the CSV file. This indicates that this step was not properly configured. 

 

Maintain Schema Mapping for NXI Schema 

If you intend to use the upload/download functionality of the File Adapter function, you need to adjust the following parameters in the SAP HANA database which as described in Adjust SAP HANA DB Parameters.  

Note: 
In maintaining the schema mapping, the physical schema will always be the owner which can be checked by logging in to the corresponding PaPM client and going to System > Status. 

 

Install SAP HANA Automated Predictive Library for Machine Learning (Optional) 

If you intend to use the Machine Learning function, you need to install SAP HANA Automated Predictive Library (APL) 1904 as the minimum version requirement in the SAP HANA database. 

Follow the procedure below to grant mandatory roles for using the Machine Learning function to the SAP DB Connection User in SAP HANA Studio as described in Install SAP HANA Automated Predictive Library for Machine Learning (Optional). 

Possible issues when not performed: 
If you do not assign these roles, you might encounter insufficient privilege issues. 

 

Default Environment Steps 

Before performing this step, ensure that all previous post-installation tasks are completed.  

The steps in this section need to be performed in SAP NetWeaver. This needs to be done for every client in the system where SAP Profitability and Performance Management will be used. 

Depending on the NetWeaver version, you need to use either Client Copy Tool SCC1 or Client Copy Tool SCC1N. Starting from SAP_BASIS 758, the transaction code SCC1 has been deprecated. Instead, the new SCC1N, which is available from SAP_BASIS 755 onwards, needs to be used. Therefore, for sample content, SCC1 can still be used if your system is below SAP_BASIS 755. However, for systems running SAP_BASIS 755 or higher, transaction code SCC1N must be used. 

Note: 
For more information on how to update the Environment Template for Default Settings SAP default environment, see SAP Note 3396866. 

Procedure:

1. If you had set up the target client by copying client “000” after installation of or upgrade to the latest support package of SAP Profitability and Performance Management 3.0 using any of the SAP_CUST-based profiles, the default SAP environment is already copied over from client “000”. You can skip the next step and go to step 3. 

2. However, if the target client already existed before installation of or upgrade to the latest support package of SAP Profitability and Performance Management 3.0 or if the client was created without a client copy from client “000” using an SAP_CUST-based profile, perform one the following steps: 

  • For SAP_BASIS versions older than 755 use transaction code SCC1 by following the procedure described in SAP_BASIS 754 or Older. 
  • For SAP_BASIS versions at 755 or newer, use transaction code SCC1N by following the procedure described in SAP_BASIS 755 or Newer. 

3. Launch transaction /NXI/P1_MODEL and check under the Sample Content node whether the delivered SAP default environment Environment Template for Default Settings exists. If it does not exist, repeat all previous steps. 

4. Launch transaction SE38, enter program /NXI/P1_GENERATE_SQLFUNCTIONS and choose Execute. Choose Execute again with the default input parameters. This results in successful log messages. 

5. If you intend to use the Machine Learning function (transaction SE38), enter program /NXI/P1_GENERATE_SQLFUNCTIONS and choose Execute. Enter /NXI/P1_GENERATE_SQLMLWRP as Include and choose Execute again. 

There you have it! Now your system is stabilized, you can already proceed in using it.  

If you have any questions, feel free to ask here under SAP Community Questions. For more blog posts, you can check the SAP Community Blog Posts. 

I hope this helps and I encourage you to share it to your friends!

entry.title: Re: MB51 - Missing values in "Amount in LC"
entry.content:
For 631w movement type, there won't be any update for inventory value in any standard tables.  You need to develop a custom report by inserting the below logic in your FS

  • Get quantity from MSEG for movement type 631 and special stock W.
  • Get price from MBEW for the material and plant.
    Multiply:
    Inventory Value = Quantity × Price (STPRS or VERPR)

entry.title: Outperforming the Pack: Getting ROI from AI in the Finance Function
entry.content:
Boston Consulting Group’s Center for CFO Excellence surveyed more than 280 finance professionals in global enterprises – and found very surprising results! 

There is a wide distribution of business outcomes associated with AI investments.

Finance teams are data-driven – and this report gives teams advice on how to proceed to get the most from their investments.  

So, what’s the secret sauce to getting the best return on AI investments in Finance?

The top answer was:

Aligning AI into finance transformation projects – if your team is already thinking about data quality and process automation and excellence at scale, this is the right time to include AI as a part of your design – not a bolted-on afterthought.

This is just 1 of 10 tactics that were shown to lead to over-performance in the ROI of AI initiatives.

Want to learn more? Check out the study "How to Get ROI from AI in the Finance Function" here.

entry.title: Governance and Quality First: How to Control and Validate Dictionary Entries in SAP Signavio
entry.content:
In business process management, consistency, clarity, and control aren't just nice to have they're essential. That’s why the way we manage and publish shared terminology in tools like SAP Signavio Process Manager matters more than ever. Whether it’s a role, system, or business term, the dictionary serves as the backbone of process transparency. But how can you ensure that these definitions are reliable, accurate, and aligned across teams?

This is where manual vs. automatic publishing and the Dictionary Doctor come into play. Together, they give organizations the flexibility to control when and how content is released, and provide the tools to systematically review and clean up entries before they reach end users. The result? A trusted, governed process landscape that supports real operational excellence.

How does it work?

 

In the SAP Signavio Process Manager you can go to Setup.

Then go to "Define notations/attributes"

Here you can pick a category and change the Publishing Mode from Automatically to Manually if you like.

If you use the manual option you see down in the feed the option to publish a revision or to revoke a revision.

If you use the automatic option you don't have this because it is done ... automatically. 

How Manual vs. Automatic Publishing Supports Governance and Quality in SAP Signavio

Being able to configure whether dictionary entries are published manually or automatically is a key feature in SAP Signavio that directly supports process governance and content quality management.

Manual Publishing: Governance through Control

Manual publishing allows organizations to enforce a review and approval process before any changes become visible in the Collaboration Hub or process models. This supports:

  • Quality Assurance: Entries can be verified, refined, and aligned with modeling conventions before release.

  • Version Management: Changes are tied to revisions, providing an audit trail and full traceability.

  • Controlled Collaboration: Teams can avoid accidental or conflicting updates by publishing changes at planned milestones.

  • Compliance Support: Manual approval ensures that sensitive business terms meet regulatory or internal standards before publication.

Use case: Ideal for large organizations, regulated industries, or projects where process documentation must go through validation cycles.

Manual publishing alone ensures that only intentional content is released, but how can you ensure that this content is actually correct, complete, and consistent?

This is where the Dictionary Doctor comes in. 

The Dictionary Doctor is a built in quality assurance tool that scans your dictionary for common issues, such as:

  • Duplicate or inconsistent entries

  • Missing mandatory attributes

  • Unused or outdated terms

  • Violations of naming conventions

 

1. Enforcing Naming Standards and Structure

Governance requires that terms especially business critical ones like roles, systems, or documents follow a consistent naming structure. The Dictionary Doctor helps by:

  • Detecting inconsistencies or deviations from naming conventions

  • Highlighting entries that are missing required fields or categories

  • Ensuring alignment with organizational terminology standards

2. Avoiding Duplicates and Redundancy

The Doctor identifies duplicate or near-duplicate entries that could confuse users or lead to inconsistent usage.
This supports governance by:

  • Maintaining a single source of truth

  • Avoiding fragmentation of definitions

  • Reducing the risk of outdated or conflicting terms

3. Validating Completeness

Incomplete dictionary entries—missing descriptions, categories, or linked elements—can erode trust in the modeling environment. The Dictionary Doctor highlights such gaps, enabling teams to fix them before publishing.

This contributes to content quality, making sure only well-defined, complete terms enter the live process landscape.

 

 

Conclusion

In short, the Dictionary Doctor enables organizations to:

  • Detect issues before they become visible to end users

  • Align content with corporate standards and policies

  • Improve the reliability of process documentation

  • Build confidence in shared terms used across models

By running the Dictionary Doctor before or after publishing a new revision, you can proactively detect and resolve issues ensuring that only validated, high-quality terms are introduced into your process landscape.

The combination of configurable publishing (manual or automatic) and systematic quality checks provides organizations using SAP Signavio with a strong foundation for achieving high-quality, consistent, and governed business process management.

If you want to test the Dictionary Doctor --> you find it in the SAP Signavio Lab Space.

About the Lab Space and how to use it you find more details here.

entry.title: User Query problem
entry.content:
HI

The  following query is run using query generator in SAP B1 HANA.

SELECT
T0."Series", N1."SeriesName", N1."Remark" AS "Prefix", T0."DocNum" AS "Invoice No.", T0."DocDate" AS "Invoice Date",
T0."CardCode", T0."CardName", T0."ShipToCode", T0."NumAtCard", T0."TaxDate" AS "PO Date", T0."GSTTranTyp",
T1."LineNum", T1."ItemCode", T1."Dscription", T1."Quantity" AS "Invoice Qty", T1."BaseQty" AS "SO Qty",
T1."BaseDocNum" AS "SO Number", T1."DocDate" AS "SO Date", T1."ShipDate" AS "SO Del Date", T1."U_Free",
T12."CityS" AS "Ship To City", T12."StateS" AS "State", T12."CountryS" AS "Country", T12."ZipCodeS" AS "ZipCode",
S1."TrnspName" AS "Shipping", T0."U_Trnspt" AS "TransPort Name", T0."U_LR_No" AS "LR Num", T0."U_LRDate" AS "LR Date", T0."U_Vehcl" AS "Vehical Num",
T0."TotalExpns" AS "Freight Charges", B1."BPLName"
FROM OINV T0
INNER JOIN INV1 T1 ON T0."DocEntry" = T1."DocEntry"
INNER JOIN INV12 T12 ON T0."DocEntry" = T12."DocEntry"
INNER JOIN NNM1 N1 ON T0."Series" = N1."Series"
INNER JOIN OBPL B1 ON T0."BPLId" = B1."BPLId"
LEFT OUTER JOIN OSHP S1 ON T0."TrnspCode" = S1."TrnspCode"
WHERE T0."DocType" = 'I'
AND T0."CANCELED" = [%0]
AND T0."DocDate" >= '[%1]'
AND T0."DocDate" <= '[%2]'
AND B1."BPLName" = '[%3]'

It is running. 

After the following statement add in select statement of above query, 

IFNULL(T0."U_PStreet", B1."Street") AS "Ship From Street", IFNULL(T0."U_PBlock", B1."Block") AS "City", IFNULL(T0."U_PCity", B1."City") AS "City",

 Now it gives the following error

[SAP AG][LIBODBCHDB DLL][HDBODBC] Connection not open;-10807 Connection down: [89006] System call 'recv' failed, rc=10054:An existing connection was forcibly closed by the remote host {0.0.0.0:64141 -> 192.168.0.224:30015 TenantName: 'Backend scheduling

what is this error ?

entry.title: Re: Variable pay based on different base dates in prorate...
entry.content:
Hi David,

Thank you for your valuable suggestion.
We can create a new pay component and generate records in it whenever there is a change in the base pay. However, the specific requirement is as follows:

If a salary change for a user occurs on 1st October, then the updated salary as of 1st October should be used for bonus calculation for the entire period from 1st August to 31st July.
If the salary change happens after 1st October (e.g., in May), then we should apply the standard proration logic:
Use the salary available as of 1st August for the period from 1st August to the date of the next salary change.
Then use the updated salary (e.g., from 1st May) for the period from 1st May to 31st July.
Based on this, I considered implementing a rule where:

If a user has a salary increase on 1st October, we create a record with the updated salary effective from 1st August in the new VP Pay component. This ensures the system uses the updated salary for the entire bonus period.
However, if there is another salary change on 1st May, then we need to adjust the logic(RQ2 in above table):
Use the old salary from 1st August to 30th September,
Use the October updated salary from 1st October to 30th April, and
Use the May updated salary from 1st May to 31st July.

I think we have complications in achieving this.
Could you please confirm if this interpretation aligns with the solution you suggested, or let me know if I’ve misunderstood any part?

Regards,
Siva

entry.title: Re: MB51 - Missing values in "Amount in LC"
entry.content:
I would not alter the standard behaviour as this could affect stock valuation.

Instead, consider enhancing MB51 to add a 'statistical value' column to the report. Look at documents such as:

  • Material Document List (MB51) Selection Screen Enhancement
  • How to Add Fields in Standard Report MB51

entry.title: API for SAP cloud ALM - Read active alerts from monitoring / operation
entry.content:
We are trying to find API in SAP cloud ALM that allows to get all active ( new) alerts from monitoring applications in Operations. For example the Health monitoring.

Can you please assist us with this task? We didn't find it in API help.

Regards

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: API for SAP cloud ALM - Read active alerts from monit...
entry.content:
Currently SAP Cloud ALM does not provide an API to extract Alert data.

Alert data is populated by the parent "Event Situation", created when the conditions to generate an event are met. These conditions are configured in the "Events" tab of the system/service configuration pop-up inside the SAP Cloud ALM for Operations use case. 

The Event Situation data (used to generate the Alert) can be sent to an external system/service by leveraging the SAP Cloud ALM External API Management functionality. When an Event Situation is generated, its data can be sent to the external system/service via the API of the external system/service. 

For more detailed information, please refer to KBA 3629828 - Extracting SAP Cloud ALM Alert data for use in another system/service

entry.title: How to Enable "Run as SAP Fiori Launchpad Sandbox" in SAP Business Application Studio?
entry.content: In SAP Web IDE, there was a convenient option called "Run as SAP Fiori Launchpad Sandbox" that allowed us to quickly test Fiori apps in a standalone sandbox environment. I'm currently using SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), and I can't find an equivalent feature.
